senses.definition: to try harder than what is expected

examples.x: She always goes the extra mile to make her clients feel valued.
The teacher went the extra mile by staying late to help struggling students.
They went the extra mile to ensure the party was a success.
The customer service team goes the extra mile to address every complaint promptly.
If you want to impress your boss, you‚Äôll need to go the extra mile on this project.

senses.definition: to put an extreme or great amount of effort into achieving or doing something

examples.x: She would go to any length to protect her family.
He went to any length to secure the promotion.
They were willing to go to any length to win the case.
The spy went to any length to gather information.
The company goes to any length to satisfy its customers.

word_list.word: [go] to the ends of the earth

senses.definition: to do whatever it takes to do or achieve something, even if it is not easy

examples.x: Going to the ends of the earth to find yourself.
Good companies are going to the ends of the earth to find good people.
I‚Äôd go to the ends of the earth to be with him.

senses.definition: to do everything one can in order to achieve a goal, particularly when it involves finding or looking for something

examples.x: He left no stone unturned in his search for his natural mother.
Since he still loves her, he is leaving no stone unturned to make her fall in love with him again.

senses.definition: to try extremely hard and do everything that one is capable of in order to succeed in something

examples.x: He had been moving heaven and earth for six weeks in order to prevent the film being made; and he had failed.
So together we moved heaven and earth to ensure, in a difficult year, that the necessary money was made available.

word_list.pronunciation: pňą äl ňąa ät ňą…Ēňźl √į…ô stňą…Ďňźps

senses.definition: to try very hard and do everything that is possible to succeed in something

examples.x: The team decided to pull out all the stops in their final match, giving their best performance and leaving nothing to chance.
In order to impress the investors, the company pulled out all the stops and presented a comprehensive business plan with innovative strategies.
The event planner pulled out all the stops to create a memorable experience, arranging stunning decorations, top-notch entertainment, and gourmet cuisine.
Determined to win the competition, she pulled out all the stops and dedicated hours to practicing, refining her skills, and seeking expert guidance.
The marketing campaign pulled out all the stops by utilizing social media, targeted advertising, celebrity endorsements, and captivating storytelling to maximize reach and impact.

word_list.pronunciation: pňą Ćnt É …ôbňĆ Ćv wňą Ćnz wňąe…™t

senses.definition: to succeed or try to succeed in doing something that often demands more power, ability, money, etc. than one has

examples.x: Singapore punches above its weight in the world economy.
The small-town mayor has been punching above her weight recently, going head-to-head with federal lawmakers over the issue of immigration reform.
Villa have punched above their weight all season and it will be a remarkable achievement if they finish fifth.

senses.definition: using all of the available resources, energy, etc.

examples.x: Dwayne and his partner Hank are working full stretch to catch what seems to be a serial killer on the loose.
Fire crews have been operating at full stretch.

senses.definition: to put in an extreme or great amount of effort to do something

examples.x: I've been bending over backward trying to help you, and this is all the thanks I get!

word_list.pronunciation: bňą Ćst wňą Ćnz ňą√¶s bňą Ćt hňą Ćmp

senses.definition: to try really hard to accomplish something

examples.x: For 10 years, he busted his hump trying to make a name for himself until getting a record deal and releasing his first disc in 1995.
I make it look easy but I busted my butt many times!
I've been busting my ass all night long to get this presentation ready for tomorrow's meeting.
So after about a year of busting our humps and missing a lot of time with our friends, we decided to go into ‚Äúobedience mode.‚ÄĚ
We're for companies on the move, organizations eager to evolve and businesses busting their asses to advance.

senses.definition: to try to do something as well as one is capable of

examples.x: Tom isn't doing his best.
They did their best to make everyone feel comfortable at the event.
You can‚Äôt always succeed, but as long as you do your best, it‚Äôs enough.
I know you‚Äôll do your best on the exam, and that‚Äôs all that matters.

senses.definition: to make effort in order to do something in the best way possible

examples.x: I was trying my utmost to remain positive and not talk about cancer.
We did our utmost to help.

senses.definition: to put in as much effort as possible into something one is doing

examples.x: Give it your all, and you won't be sorry later.
It wasn't easy being a substitute father but Harry gave it his all.

senses.definition: to use all of one's energy, resources, determination, or strength to do something

examples.x: The team went all out for a win.

word_list.pronunciation: …°ňĆo ä ňĆa ä…ĺ…ôv wňą Ćnz wňąe…™

senses.definition: to put in more effort than what is expected or required of one in order to do or achieve something

examples.x: She can be excessively generous and will go out of her way to help others.

word_list.pronunciation: pňĆ ät wňą Ćnz bňą…õst fňą ät fňąoňź…Ļw…öd

senses.definition: to put as much effort as possible into doing something, particularly something difficult

examples.x: I am putting my best foot forward to meet this deadline.
During the job interview, she aimed to put her best foot forward by dressing professionally and highlighting her skills.
Before meeting their future in-laws, many people try to put their best foot forward to make a good impression.
In any competition, it's crucial to put your best foot forward to demonstrate your skills and abilities.
The artist displayed their artwork with the intent to put their best foot forward and gain recognition.

senses.definition: used to state that even if someone failed to succeed, they tried their best

examples.x: Not all is perfect, but it isn't for want of trying.
The first half was scoreless, but not for the want of trying as the two teams combined for 14 shots.

senses.definition: to make effort to do something in the best possible way

examples.x: Give me your best shot, hold nothing back.
He ultimately failed, but I gotta say that he gave me his best shot.
I just have a feeling that we have given it our best shot.

senses.definition: to do everything that one is capable of in order to do or achieve something

examples.x: He went to the wall to defend his beliefs.
These guys will go to the wall for you.

senses.definition: the best or most possible thing or action that can help one achieve success in something

examples.x: When it comes to investing, a diversified portfolio is often considered the best bet for long-term growth.
Seeking professional advice is the best bet for navigating complex legal matters.

senses.definition: to try one's best to do or achieve something

examples.x: She busted a gut trying to finish the project on time.
The team worked tirelessly and busted a gut to deliver the report on time.
I had to bust a gut just to keep up with the workload.
She busted a gut preparing for the final exams, but it paid off.
Lifting those heavy boxes all day, he really busted a gut.
